right_target.finding: OpenTargets returns 3438 total melanoma evidences for AKT1, but the top rows are all europepmc literature co-mentions (score 1, PMIDs 24178756, 23443536, 34711805, 20711233) plus one cancer_biomarkers affected-pathway row for cutaneous melanoma (PMID 24265152). No genetic/somatic-mutation datasource ties AKT1 directly to melanoma; GWAS returns 6 AKT1 SNPs (e.g. rs114194742, intron_variant, 14q32.33) and 0 map to any melanoma trait. AKT1 is a downstream PI3K/AKT effector, not a melanoma driver.

right_tissue.finding: CellMarker_search_by_gene for AKT1 (Human) returns only 1 record: a Cancer cell (T helper 2 cell) in Uterine cervix. No melanoma/skin/melanocyte tissue marker record exists for AKT1, so it provides no melanoma tissue-selectivity signal.

right_safety.finding: CellMarker shows no normal-tissue marker record for AKT1 (only 1 cancer-cell record), so no normal-cell breadth can be established from it; however AKT1 is a broadly expressed housekeeping PI3K/AKT survival kinase (Pharos: mediates growth-factor neuronal survival; Proteus syndrome mutations), implying wide normal-tissue on-target liability. OpenFDA drug events for the AKT inhibitor GSK2141795 (uprosertib, from the melanoma trial) returned 1 report with reactions including fatigue, hypersensitivity, confusional state, maculo-papular rash, syncope, dehydration.
right_safety.provenance: CellMarker normal cells (no normal record for AKT1); Pharos_get_target (AKT1); OpenFDA_search_drug_events (GSK2141795): 1 report

right_patient.finding: search_clinical_trials (intervention=AKT1, condition=melanoma) returns 1 trial: NCT01941927, phase 2, COMPLETED, trametinib + the pan-AKT inhibitor GSK2141795 in BRAF wild-type melanoma (est. 48 patients). AKT is targeted only in combination as a downstream node, not as a stratifying biomarker; no AKT1-mutation-selected melanoma patient population is enrolled.

right_commercial.finding: Only 1 melanoma trial names an AKT inhibitor (NCT01941927, completed phase 2 combination), and TheraSAbDab_search_by_target (AKT1) returns 0 therapeutic antibodies (AKT1 is intracellular, not an antibody target). No dedicated AKT1-in-melanoma commercial program; competitive/differentiation whitespace is thin and mechanistically it is a crowded generic PI3K/AKT node.

tractability.finding: Pharos_get_target (AKT1) reports TDL=Tchem (has small-molecule ligands but not an approved drug), family=Kinase, UniProt P31749, novelty 3.185e-05 (very low, i.e. heavily studied), publicationCount 3360. Highly druggable kinase with chemical matter, but low novelty and intracellular location rule out antibody modalities.
